residual_risks: The two-item cohort supports only the preregistered proof-of-mechanism decision to open a separate target A/B; it provides no target-effect estimate or external generalization.
Both sources overlap the official corpus, so source familiarity or memorization remains possible, although this is disclosed and does not violate the stated planner isolation.

Semantic novelty and exhaustive enumeration of alternative minimal support sets still depend on the two independent reviewers' judgment, leaving ordinary reviewer-error risk despite fail-closed handling and zero retries.

    "Two-question cohort gives a narrow, low-power signal; a lucky 2/2 GO is possible even if the planner mechanism is marginal. Acceptable only because GO merely authorizes a separate frozen target A/B with zero fact credit, so a false-positive GO is caught downstream rather than converted into runtime credit.",
    "Semantic novelty of replacement questions is asserted by author/cross-review rather than any registry check; a subtle S99 semantic-equivalent slipping through would soften the novelty claim, though the duplicate-is-NO-GO rule and pixel-grounded independent authorship bound this to conservative failure, not false GO.",
    "Both sources overlap the official corpus, so the experiment cannot detect memorized-source advantage; the brief correctly refrains from claiming source independence, but any interpretation of GO must not be extended to unseen-source performance.",
    "Sol authors and Fable cross-reviews within the same eight-call envelope; correlated blind spots between the two Frontier models could pass an unsupported fact. Mitigated by the exact support-mapping equality requirement and deterministic v4 fail-closed validation, but not eliminated.",
    "Selected-unit precision >=0.80 on a 25-unit packet with only two questions means a single extraneous-but-adjacent unit selection can still pass; precision is a weak discriminator at this n, and 100% atomic fact-support recall plus 2/2 completeness carry the real decision weight.",
    "Planner at reasoning 'low' with zero retries may produce a provider-side incompleteness classified HOLD; the HOLD/NO-GO boundary depends on correctly distinguishing model incompleteness from semantic failure, which is procedurally defined but still requires honest classification at seal time."
